evals.name: springboot-java-maven-happy-path
evals.prompt: Setup MongoDBSyncTargetSystem for my Spring Boot Java app using Maven.
evals.expected_output: A complete Spring Boot @Bean configuration for MongoDBSyncTargetSystem with Maven dependencies and canonical placeholders.

evals.name: standalone-kotlin-gradle-new-client
evals.prompt: I need to configure Flamingock for a standalone Kotlin project with Gradle. I don't have a MongoClient yet.
evals.expected_output: A Kotlin snippet creating both a MongoClient and the MongoDBSyncTargetSystem, plus Gradle plugin configuration.
expectations: Uses TODO for unresolved connection/database values
Configures the flamingock plugin with mongodb() module

evals.name: springboot-java-existing-client-reuse
evals.prompt: Flamingock is already in my Spring Boot app and I already have a MongoClient bean. Wire MongoDBSyncTargetSystem without creating another client.
evals.expected_output: A Spring Boot MongoDBSyncTargetSystem bean that reuses the existing MongoClient bean and does not generate a duplicate client.
expectations: Does not create a second MongoClient bean
Stays in the targetsystem skill boundary

evals.name: standalone-java-multiple-client-ambiguity
evals.prompt: I have two MongoClient variables in my standalone Java app. Help me wire MongoDBSyncTargetSystem.
evals.expected_output: A response that flags the multiple-client ambiguity, asks for the intended client or shows how to choose one explicitly, without inventing a selection.
expectations: Does not silently choose one client
Keeps the answer focused on MongoDBSyncTargetSystem wiring

evals.name: targetsystem-hand-off-to-change-skill
evals.prompt: My MongoDBSyncTargetSystem is already configured. Now create a migration to add an index to users.
evals.expected_output: Either a hand-off to the MongoDB sync change skill or migration-oriented help that assumes the TargetSystem is already configured and does not pretend onboarding is needed.
expectations: Either redirects to flamingock-mongodb-sync-change or gives migration-oriented help for the requested change
Does not pretend onboarding or targetsystem setup is still needed
Keeps the response focused on migration authoring rather than redoing targetsystem wiring
